leads.name: Open Letter Books
leads.title: Publisher
leads.subj: Printing your translation list -- short runs from Ann Arbor
leads.opener: This is an introduction, nothing more. Open Letter is a literary press working in translation, and presses like yours are the work we do most. We would like to be on the list of printers you quote when your next season is ready. That is the entire ask.
leads.b3: Backlist reprints in small quantities, so your library collections stay in stock without filling a warehouse.
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary translation press housed at the University of Rochester, publishing about 10 titles a year per their own about page. They sell backlist collections direct to libraries, which means recurring small reprint runs rather than one large annual print buy.
leads.pitch: Translation press with a library-direct backlist model that generates steady short reprint work.
leads.drafted_email:
Dear Chad,

This is an introduction, nothing more. Open Letter is a literary press working in translation, and presses like yours are the work we do most. We would like to be on the list of printers you quote when your next season is ready. That is the entire ask.

I'm reaching out from Cushing-Malloy in Ann Arbor, Michigan. We've been manufacturing books for independent and university presses since 1948.

Here's what makes us different:

  • One person manages your project start to finish: same contact for quoting, production, proofing, and delivery. Nothing falls through the cracks.
  • 20 working days from approved files to finished books, delivered to your door.
  • Backlist reprints in small quantities, so your library collections stay in stock without filling a warehouse.

Not sure where to start? That's exactly why we're here. We'd love to talk through your options and provide a quote for your next book. To get started, just reply to this email or click https://cushing-malloy.com/request-quote/ to submit your details.

Warm regards,

Steve Olko
Director of Sales
solko@cushing-malloy.com
P: (810) 296-7133
Cushing-Malloy, Inc.  |  1350 N. Main, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
cushing-malloy.com   –  Where Books Are Bound For Greatness®

leads.name: Arrowsmith Press
leads.title: Editor and Publisher
leads.subj: Printing your poetry and translation titles -- Ann Arbor short runs
leads.opener: Printers usually reach literary presses by referral, and we would rather earn the introduction than wait for it. Arrowsmith is an independent press publishing poetry and translation. That is the work we manufacture most weeks of the year. We would like the chance to quote a title for you.
leads.b3: Accented and non-Latin character sets print cleanly, which matters on a translation list.
leads.notes: Independent press publishing international poetry and translation with a substantial Ukrainian and Middle Eastern list, and administers the Derek Walcott Prize. Multiple 2026 titles are live on their catalog. GENERIC ADDRESS FLAG: only a shared gmail address is published; Askold Melnyczuk is the named publisher.
leads.pitch: Translation heavy poetry press where typographic accuracy is a real purchasing criterion.
leads.drafted_email:

leads.name: Empty Bowl
leads.title: Editor and Publisher
leads.subj: Printing your 50th anniversary anthology -- Ann Arbor short runs
leads.opener: Empty Bowl has a 50th anniversary anthology, Working the Land, Working the Sea, open for submissions. Congratulations on reaching fifty years. An anniversary volume is usually a bigger production decision than a standard title. We would like to quote it.
leads.b3: Anniversary and commemorative editions handled as their own project, including special binding options.
leads.notes: Cascadia region poetry, anthology and translation publisher marking its 50th anniversary in fall 2026 with the anthology Working the Land, Working the Sea. Received a Humanities Washington anniversary award. ROLE ADDRESS FLAG: editor@ is the published address at a two person press, so it does reach Holly Hughes directly.
leads.pitch: Fifty year old regional press with a named anniversary anthology in production planning now.
leads.drafted_email:

leads.name: Front Edge Publishing
leads.title: Founding Editor
leads.subj: Printing your nonprofit and author titles -- a manufacturer down the road
leads.opener: This is an introduction from a neighbor. Front Edge Publishing works out of Canton and we are in Ann Arbor, about half an hour away. Independent publishers are the majority of who we manufacture for. We would like to be quoted on your next title.
leads.b3: Close enough for in person proofing, and short runs sized to a single nonprofit partner's order.
leads.notes: Canton MI publisher specializing in religion, spirituality, interfaith, health and nonprofit partnered books, founded 2007. Roughly 35 minutes from Ann Arbor, the second strongest proximity score in this run. Their nonprofit partnered custom runs are naturally repeat short run business. GENERIC ADDRESS FLAG: only info@ is published; David Crumm is the named founding editor.
leads.pitch: Michigan neighbor whose nonprofit partnered custom books are recurring short run work.
leads.drafted_email:

leads.name: Eckhartz Press
leads.title: Publisher and co-founder
leads.subj: Printing your Chicago titles -- short runs from Ann Arbor
leads.opener: This is an introduction, and the ask is simple. Eckhartz Press is an independent Chicago publisher, and small presses are the majority of who we manufacture for. We would like to quote your next title. That is all this note is for.
leads.b3: Reprints on short notice when a local title moves faster than expected.
leads.notes: Chicago interest boutique press covering local media history, sports and memoir, self described as a small press with limited staff. The 7th edition of EveryCubEver was released in 2025, showing an active reprint and revision cycle. Chicago is about 240 miles from Ann Arbor.
leads.pitch: Chicago micro press with a proven multi edition title and a live reprint cycle.
leads.drafted_email:
